document_summary: Career development plan by Dehuan Liu for after immigrating to the United States. Plans include: (1) visiting scholar at University of Pittsburgh with Prof. Lingfei Wu for data analysis research; (2) academic exchanges with Harvard East Asian studies scholars; (3) collaboration with multiple US universities (Indiana, Missouri, Washington, Michigan State, Miami) for joint training programs; (4) research commercialization at Boston College; (5) market research consulting for American companies entering East Asian markets, leveraging CMRA presidency and Fortune 500 client experience; (6) religious and cultural exchange activities.

snippets.text: My name is Dehuan Liu, and I am currently a professor and associate dean at Peking University. I hope to immigrate to the United States in order to further develop my research and professional expertise. After immigrating to the United States, I plan to continue working in the field of social science research methods and collaborate with colleagues in the American academic community. My goal is to improve and enhance social science research methods in the United States and promote academic exchange and collaboration.
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Liu's statement of intent to continue social science research methods work and collaborate with American academic community.

snippets.text: 1. I will accept an invitation from Professor Lingfei Wu of University of Pittsburgh (with whom I have jointly published papers) to be a visiting scholar at the School of Computing and Information. During my visit, we will engage in academic research collaboration, particularly in the field of data analysis, taking on the responsibility of data modeling. We will conduct in-depth research on data analysis methods and aim to co-publish high-level research papers. Additionally, while at the University of Pittsburgh, I will also explore data analysis models with Professor Xinghua Lu, an expert in biostatistics, and attempt to collaborate with him.
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Specific plan: visiting scholar at University of Pittsburgh for data analysis research with Prof. Lingfei Wu and biostatistics collaboration with Prof. Xinghua Lu.

snippets.text: 3. I have represented Peking University and collaborated with Professor James Shanahan from Indiana University to promote and facilitate the joint training program between the Media School of Indiana University and the School of New Media of Peking University. Therefore, I have extensive experience in talent cultivation and international cooperation. I plan to collaborate with more universities in the United States, especially in the field of media studies. I intend to engage in discussions and exchanges with Professor Yvonne Wu from the University of Missouri, Associate Professor Huatong Sun from the University of Washington, Professor Hairong Li from Michigan State University, and Professor Cong Li from the University of Miami, to explore joint talent
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Plans to expand US university collaborations in media studies with professors at Missouri, Washington, Michigan State, and Miami, building on existing Indiana University-PKU joint program.

snippets.text: 5. Due to my previous role as the President of the China Marketing Research Association (CMRA) for two terms, I have led multiple multinational companies such as Nielsen, Kantar, Ipsos, GFK, and domestic market research institutions like Zero Point in expanding into the Chinese market. I am one of the most important experts in the field of market research in China. I have provided market research services to top Chinese companies such as Tencent, Alibaba, Baidu, NetEase, Sina, Sohu, Xiaomi, Vanke, Poly, and Mini Snow Ice City, as well as consulting services to international brands such as Procter & Gamble. In recent years, the automotive companies I have served include NIO, MG, Volkswagen, Mercedes-Benz, Volvo, Toyota, Great Wall, Blue Valley, and Changan Ford. I have a profound understanding of the Chinese market and a unique insight into consumer behavior. After immigrating to the United States, my plan is to collaborate with American companies and provide professional consulting and market research services to facilitate their healthy development in the East Asian market.
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Plans to provide market research consulting for US companies entering East Asian markets, leveraging CMRA presidency and extensive client experience with Fortune 500 companies (Nielsen, Kantar, P&G, NIO, Mercedes-Benz, Volvo, Toyota, etc.).

snippets.text: In the past thirty years, I have been engaged in the exploration and practice of social science research methods, and have achieved great respect from academia and industry. In the field of digital marketing, my ISMAS model (Interest, Search, Mouth, Action, Share) for consumer engagement with marketing information (advertisements), which I created, has gained wide recognition from academia and industry. It has become a mainstream cognitive model in the advertising and digital marketing industries. As a renowned scholar and expert in research methods, I am able to collaborate with American universities and companies in multiple areas to jointly promote the development and advancement of the social sciences, and to facilitate mutually beneficial cooperation and exchange between China and the United States.
snippets.subject: Dehuan Liu
snippets.reasoning: Summarizes Liu's 30-year career and ISMAS model as mainstream cognitive model in advertising, positioning his ability to contribute to US-China academic and industry collaboration.

entities.name: Dehuan Liu
entities.identity: Professor and Associate Dean at Peking University; creator of ISMAS model; former CMRA President
